action.openDataDir: Open log file directory in file manager

error.tokenExpired: Access token expired, please obtain a new one

filter.gridPlaceholder: Enter a grid, e.g. FN31 or FN31AB
filter.gridHelp: Supports 4/6/8-character grid prefix matching, so FN31 also matches FN31AB.

stats.dxccDeletedHelp: This includes {{count}} historical DXCC entities that were deleted from the DXCC list. These are not QSOs you deleted manually.
stats.dxccNoBucketData: No DXCC bucket data is available yet.

globe.title: Last 24 Hours Global QSOs
globe.subtitle: Positions are estimated from grid centers; v1 uses a muted land-only basemap.
globe.loadingCurrentPage: Rendering globe view for the current page…
globe.error: Failed to load recent globe activity
globe.empty: No recent QSO with a valid grid was found in the last 24 hours.
globe.emptyCurrentPage: No QSO with a valid grid is available on the current filtered page.
globe.missingHome: No usable home location was found. Configure the operator grid or station coordinates/grid first.
globe.missingHomeCurrentPage: Your station location is still missing, so the globe cannot draw the paths yet. Add an operator grid or station location first.
globe.workedGridEmpty: No worked grids are available for the current condition.

summary.invalidGrid: {{count}} recent QSOs were skipped because their grids were invalid.
summary.boundaryNote: V1 renders muted land polygons only and intentionally omits country borders.

provider.fullSyncDesc: Download then upload, bidirectional full sync

qrz.uploadDesc: Upload local QSOs to QRZ.com Logbook

lotw.downloadConfirmationsDesc: Download LoTW confirmation status and import missing local QSOs
lotw.downloadResult: LoTW download complete: {{downloaded}} remote records, {{updated}} local QSOs updated, {{imported}} missing QSOs imported
lotw.dateModalDesc: Select the start date for this LoTW confirmation download. It defaults to the last download time; if there is no history yet, it falls back to the last 30 days.
lotw.noLastDownloadHint: No previous download found; defaulted to the last 30 days.
lotw.selectDateRequired: Please choose a sync start date
lotw.uploadDesc: Use uploaded .p12 certificates so TX-5DR can sign on the server and upload directly to LoTW
lotw.uploadModalDesc: Before uploading, confirm that a usable certificate is available, the upload location is complete, and there are QSOs waiting to be uploaded.
lotw.uploadReadySummary: The basic upload requirements are satisfied.
lotw.uploadNeedsConfigSummary: Some upload prerequisites are still missing. Please fix the items below first.

export.allDesc: Export every QSO record in this logbook.
export.rangeDesc: Export only QSO records within the selected UTC date range.
export.dateRangeDesc: The end date includes records through 23:59:59.999 UTC on that day.
export.dateRangeRequired: Select both a start date and an end date.
export.includeFiltersDesc: When enabled, the current callsign, grid, band, mode, and confirmation filters are applied too. It is off by default to avoid accidental scoped exports.
export.noFiltersDesc: No page filters are currently active, so there are no filters to apply to this export.

import.guideDesc: Before importing, please confirm the file format and content meet the requirements. Recognized records will be merged into the current logbook.
import.adifDesc: Supports standard .adi / .adif files containing ADIF records.
import.csvDesc: Only CSV files exported by TX-5DR are supported. Arbitrary third-party CSV files are not supported.
import.requirementAdifFields: ADIF files should include key fields such as CALL, QSO_DATE, and TIME_ON. Records missing required fields will be skipped.
import.requirementCsvHeaders: CSV files must keep the TX-5DR export headers, including at least Date, Time, Callsign, Frequency (MHz), and Mode.
import.requirementMerge: If the same QSO already exists, the system will fill missing fields and upgrade confirmation status when better data is available, instead of simply appending duplicates.
import.errorFallback: Import failed, please try again later

editQso.title: Edit QSO Record
editQso.dxccMissing: No DXCC entity has been resolved for this QSO yet.
editQso.editWarning: Modifying QSO records may affect the accuracy of statistics. Please proceed with caution.

addQso.title: Add QSO Record
addQso.subtitle: Manually enter a new contact record

deleteQso.title: Delete QSO Record
deleteQso.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete the QSO with {{callsign}}?
deleteQso.warning: This action cannot be undone. The record will be permanently deleted.

logbookSyncSettings.title: Logbook Sync
logbookSyncSettings.description: Configure sync settings for various amateur radio logging services
logbookSyncSettings.wavelogDesc: Open-source amateur radio logging system with bidirectional sync, real-time upload and batch download
logbookSyncSettings.qrzDesc: Global amateur radio operator database for contact information lookup and verification
logbookSyncSettings.lotwDesc: ARRL official QSL confirmation system for award applications and contact verification
logbookSyncSettings.wavelogTabDesc: Amateur radio logging service with bidirectional sync

lotwSettings.title: LoTW Sync Settings
lotwSettings.description: Use your LoTW account for confirmation downloads, and upload a .p12 certificate once so TX-5DR can sign QSOs on the server.
lotwSettings.fillUsernamePassword: Please fill in username and password first
lotwSettings.certificateIntro: This follows the Wavelog-style workflow: upload a .p12 certificate exported from TQSL once, then let TX-5DR sign and upload on the server.
lotwSettings.certificateStep1: Find the active LoTW certificate in TQSL.
lotwSettings.certificateStep2: Export it as a .p12 file and do not set a password during export.
lotwSettings.certificateStep3: Upload the .p12 file here so TX-5DR can read the callsign, DXCC, and validity window automatically.
lotwSettings.certificateStep4: Complete the upload location fields, then use manual upload or enable auto-upload.
lotwSettings.certificateEmpty: No LoTW certificates have been imported yet.
lotwSettings.locationIntro: These fields participate in LoTW signing. Fill in the real station location details, not just the callsign.
lotwSettings.locationCallsignDesc: It must match the my-callsign value stored in the local QSOs you are uploading.
lotwSettings.locationDxccDesc: Usually auto-filled after certificate import, but you can adjust it manually if needed.
lotwSettings.locationStateDesc: If this DXCC requires {{label}}, it must match the station location tied to the certificate.
lotwSettings.locationCountyDesc: If this DXCC requires {{label}}, use the standard value accepted by LoTW.
lotwSettings.autoUploadDesc: Only available when the certificate and upload location are complete; newly saved QSOs will be signed and uploaded automatically.
lotwSettings.uploadReadySummary: The basic LoTW upload check passed.
lotwSettings.uploadPendingSummary: The LoTW upload configuration is still incomplete.
lotwSettings.usage1: Confirmation downloads only require your LoTW username and password.
lotwSettings.usage2: Log uploads no longer depend on a local TQSL executable; they depend on the .p12 certificate you import into TX-5DR.
lotwSettings.usage3: If certificate import fails, the most common reason is that the exported .p12 still has a password.
lotwSettings.usage4: If upload fails, check certificate validity, QSO date range, and station location fields first.

guidance.export_unprotected_p12: Export an unprotected .p12 certificate from TQSL, then upload it to TX-5DR.
guidance.configure_station_location: LoTW upload requires complete station-location fields, not just the callsign.
guidance.open_settings_and_upload_certificate: Open LoTW settings first and upload a usable .p12 certificate.

serverError.lotw_connection_success: LoTW connection succeeded and the credentials can be used to download confirmations.
serverError.lotw_credentials_missing: Please fill in your LoTW username and password first.
serverError.lotw_auth_failed: Your LoTW username or password is incorrect.
serverError.lotw_response_invalid: LoTW returned an unexpected response. Please try again later.
serverError.lotw_network_timeout: Connection to the LoTW server timed out. Please check your network and try again.
serverError.lotw_network_failed: Unable to reach the LoTW server. Please check your network and try again.
serverError.lotw_connection_failed: LoTW connection failed. Please try again later.
serverError.lotw_upload_sign_failed: Signing failed before upload. Please check whether the certificate is still valid.
serverError.lotw_upload_rejected: The LoTW server rejected this upload. Please verify the certificate, station location, and log fields.
serverError.lotw_upload_batch_failed: LoTW upload failed. Please review the certificate, network, and upload-location settings, then try again.
serverError.lotw_not_configured: LoTW is not fully configured for this callsign yet. Please set the account, certificate, and upload location first.
serverError.certificate_file_required: Please choose a .p12 certificate file first.
serverError.certificate_password_protected: This .p12 certificate is password protected. Please export it from TQSL again without a password.
serverError.certificate_invalid: This certificate file could not be parsed. Please confirm it is a LoTW .p12 certificate exported from TQSL.
serverError.certificate_not_found: The selected LoTW certificate was not found. It may already have been removed.

issue.certificate_missing: No LoTW certificate is currently available for upload.
issue.upload_location_callsign_missing: The LoTW upload callsign is still empty.
issue.upload_location_dxcc_missing: The LoTW upload DXCC is still empty.
issue.upload_location_grid_missing: The LoTW upload grid square is still empty.
issue.upload_location_cq_missing: The LoTW upload CQ zone is still empty.
issue.upload_location_itu_missing: The LoTW upload ITU zone is still empty.
issue.upload_location_state_missing: The required state/province/prefecture field is still missing for this DXCC.
issue.upload_location_county_missing: The required county/region field is still missing for this DXCC.
issue.qso_callsign_missing: Some QSOs are missing the station callsign and cannot be uploaded.
issue.qso_callsign_mismatch: Some QSOs belong to a different station callsign than the current LoTW upload configuration.
issue.certificate_date_range_mismatch: Some QSOs fall outside the signing date ranges of the imported certificates.
issue.no_pending_qsos: There are no QSOs waiting to be uploaded right now.

qrzSettings.title: QRZ.com Logbook Sync Settings
qrzSettings.description: Sync your logbook with QRZ.com Logbook
qrzSettings.apiKeyDesc: Get API Key from QRZ.com → My Logbook → Settings
qrzSettings.fillApiKey: Please fill in API Key first
qrzSettings.autoUploadDesc: Automatically upload to QRZ.com Logbook after each successful QSO
qrzSettings.downloadSyncDesc: For download and manual sync, please use the Logbook page.
qrzSettings.usage1: QRZ.com Logbook API requires XML Logbook Data subscription

wavelogSettings.title: WaveLog Sync Settings
wavelogSettings.description: Configure connection and sync options for WaveLog server
wavelogSettings.solution1: Check if WaveLog server is running
wavelogSettings.solution2: Verify URL format is correct (e.g., http://192.168.1.100:8086)
wavelogSettings.solution3: Check network connection and firewall settings
wavelogSettings.solution4: Try accessing the WaveLog URL directly in a browser
wavelogSettings.networkSolution1: Check if network connection is stable
wavelogSettings.networkSolution2: Verify WaveLog server is responding normally
wavelogSettings.networkSolution3: Check if firewall is blocking the connection
wavelogSettings.urlSolution3: If using IP address, verify the IP is correct
wavelogSettings.urlDesc: Full WaveLog server address including protocol (https://)
wavelogSettings.apiKeyDesc: Generate in WaveLog right menu → API Keys
wavelogSettings.fillUrlApiKey: Please fill in WaveLog URL and API key first
wavelogSettings.stationDesc: Select the Station configuration created in WaveLog
wavelogSettings.radioNameDesc: Radio device name displayed in WaveLog
wavelogSettings.autoUploadDesc: Automatically upload to WaveLog server after each successful QSO
wavelogSettings.downloadSyncDesc: Auto download sync is not available. Please use the manual sync button on the Logbook page to download and sync QSO records from WaveLog.
wavelogSettings.usage1: WaveLog server must have HTTPS enabled to use this feature
wavelogSettings.usage2: View Station ID in WaveLog's Station Locations
wavelogSettings.usage3: Generate API key in WaveLog's API Keys
wavelogSettings.usage4: Uploaded QSO data uses ADIF format
wavelogSettings.usage5: For download sync, use the manual sync button on the Logbook page

logbookPage.paramErrorDesc: Sorry, unable to open the logbook page. Missing required operator ID parameter. Please reopen from the operator panel on the main interface.
logbookPage.hint: Tip: Click the logbook button in the main interface operator panel to correctly open the logbook
